blocks:
Sūrat al-Ḥujurāt (The Private Apartments)

Medinan, its number of verses is eighteen verses by consensus (بالإجماع)* بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ*** (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لا تُقَدِّمُوا ** (1) O you who have faith! Do not go ahead And it was read with the opening of the tā (بفتح التاء), meaning an order (امراً), or yourselves (أنفسكم), or do not go ahead (لا تتقدموا). From this (root) is the vanguard (مقدّمة) of the army, referring to those who go ahead (لمتقدّميهم). بَيْنَ يَدَيِ اللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ ** before Allah and His Apostle. It was said that the meaning is: Do not decide an affair before He and His Apostle judge concerning it. And it was said: Do not go ahead in walking, and what is meant is before the Apostle of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. And the mention of Allah is an exaltation of Him and an indication that He holds a position with Allah that necessitates His veneration. وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ (and be wary of Allah) concerning the going ahead (or deciding first). إِنَّ اللهَ سَمِيعٌ ** Indeed Allah is all-hearing, (all-hearing) of your sayings. عَلِيمٌ ** all-knowing. (all-knowing) of your actions.

(2) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لا تَرْفَعُوا أَصْواتَكُمْ فَوْقَ صَوْتِ النَّبِيِ (2) O you who have faith! Do not raise your voices above the voice of the Prophet, Meaning: When you speak to Him, do not let your voices exceed His voice. وَلا تَجْهَرُوا لَهُ بِالْقَوْلِ كَجَهْرِ بَعْضِكُمْ لِبَعْضٍ and do not speak aloud to him, as you speak aloud to one another, And do not reach with Him the loudness (الجهر) that circulates among you. Rather, make your voices lower than His voice, out of protection for the welcome (الترحيب) and observance of proper conduct (الأدب). The repetition of the address (النداء) is to call for increased insight (الاستبصار), exaggeration in awakening (الإيقاظ), indication of the independence of the one being addressed (المنادى له), and increased attention to Him. أَنْ تَحْبَطَ أَعْمالُكُمْ lest your works should fail, [This is] out of dislike that your works should fail, or so that they fail وَأَنْتُمْ لا تَشْعُرُونَ (while you are unaware) that they are failing (محبطة).

In al-Qummi, [it is narrated that] it was revealed concerning the delegation of Banu Tamim. When they arrived before the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, they would stand at the door of His chamber and call out: "O Muhammad, come out to us!" And when the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله came out, they would walk ahead of Him. And when they spoke to Him, they would raise their voices above His voice, saying: "O Muhammad, O Muhammad, what do you say about such-and-such?" just as they speak to one another. So, Allah revealed the Ayah.

In al-Jawami', narrated from Ibn Abbas, [it is stated that] it was revealed concerning Thabit ibn Qays ibn Shammas. He had deafness (وقر) in his ear and had a loud voice (جهوريّ الصوت). Thus, when he spoke to Him, he would raise his voice, and sometimes the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله was bothered by His voice. He said: And it is narrated that when the Ayah was revealed, Thabit was missed. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله inquired about him and was informed of his situation. So, He called him and asked him. Thabit said: "O Messenger of Allah, this Ayah has been revealed, and I have a loud voice (جهوريّ الصوت), so I fear that my work has failed (حبط)." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "You are not [one of those intended] there, for you shall live well and die well, and you are one of the people of Paradise."

In Tafsir al-Imam عليه السلام, regarding Surah Al-Baqarah, concerning the Almighty's saying: لا تَقُولُوا راعِنا وَقُولُوا انْظُرْنا. (Do not say, 'Ra‘ina,' but say, 'Unzurna,') Narrated from al-Kazim عليه السلام: When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله arrived in Medina, and the Emigrants (المهاجرون) and the Helpers (الأنصار) multiplied around Him, and the questions directed to Him increased, they used to address Him with great address (الخطاب العظيم) which was not befitting for Him. This was because Allah the Almighty had said: يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لا تَرْفَعُوا أَصْواتَكُمْ فَوْقَ صَوْتِ النَّبِيِ (O you who have faith! Do not raise your voices above the voice of the Prophet,) —the Ayah. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله was merciful toward them, compassionate toward them, and striving diligently to remove sins (الآثام) from them, to the extent that He would look at the one addressing Him and deliberately make His voice higher than the speaker's voice, so as to remove from him what Allah had warned against concerning the failure of his works (إحباط أعماله). Even a Bedouin (أعرابيّ) man once called Him from behind a wall with a loud voice (جهوريّ), saying: "O Muhammad!" So, He answered him with a voice higher than his, desiring that the Bedouin should not sin due to the raising of his voice.

(٣) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَغُضُّونَ أَصْواتَهُمْ They lower them عِنْدَ رَسُولِ اللهِ out of consideration for proper conduct (الأدب) أُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ امْتَحَنَ اللهُ قُلُوبَهُمْ لِلتَّقْوى He tested them for it and trained them upon it لَهُمْ مَغْفِرَةٌ for their sins وَأَجْرٌ عَظِيمٌ for their lowering (of voices) and the rest of their acts of obedience (طاعاتهم). And the indefiniteness (of the reward) is for magnification (التعظيم).

(3) Indeed, those who lower their voices in the presence of the Apostle of Allah—it is they whose hearts Allah has tested for piety. For them is forgiveness and a great reward.

(٤) ** إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُنادُونَكَ مِنْ وَراءِ الْحُجُراتِ ** (Indeed, those who call out to you from behind the apartments) From outside of them, behind them, or in front of them. And what is meant are the apartments (حجرات) of his wives صلى الله عليه وآله. أَكْثَرُهُمْ لا يَعْقِلُونَ ** (most of them do not exercise their reason) since reason necessitates good manners (حسن الأدب) and observing respect (مراعاة الحشمة) for one who holds this rank (المنصب).

(٥) وَلَوْ أَنَّهُمْ صَبَرُوا حَتَّى تَخْرُجَ إِلَيْهِمْ لَكانَ خَيْراً لَهُمْ ** (5) Had they been patient until you came out to them, it would have been better for them. than the haste and the calling, due to what it contains of preserving etiquette and honoring the Messenger, which necessitate praise, reward, and the granting of what is requested. In [the word] إِلَيْهِمْ (to them) there is an indication that even if he came out, it would not be specifically for their sake. They ought to be patient until he initiates the conversation with them or turns his attention to them. وَاللهُ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ ** And Allah is All-forgiving, All-merciful since He confined [His action] to advice and reprimand for those who behaved badly and abandoned the honoring of the Messenger.

(٦) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِنْ جاءَكُمْ فاسِقٌ بِنَبَإٍ فَتَبَيَّنُوا (6) (O you who have faith! If a vicious person brings you a report, verify it) meaning, ascertain and investigate. And it was read with the three-dotted thā (ث) and the single-dotted bā (ب) [i.e., فتثبّتوا] from al-tathabbut (التثبت) (verification/firmness). And he attributed it in al-Majma' to al-Baqir (peace be upon him), meaning: so stop until the situation becomes clear. أَنْ تُصِيبُوا (lest you afflict) lest you afflict a people out of ignorance (بِجَهالَةٍ), being ignorant of their situation, فَتُصْبِحُوا so that you become عَلى ما فَعَلْتُمْ نادِمِينَ (regretful over what you have done) afflicted by a lasting sorrow, wishing that it had not occurred.

It is narrated that the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم sent Walid ibn 'Uqbah as a collector of ṣadaqah (مصدّقاً) to Banu al-Mustaliq. There was enmity (احنَة) between him and them. When they heard of him, they went out to meet him, but he assumed they were coming to fight him. So he returned and said to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "They have apostatized and withheld the zakāt." So He intended to fight them, and then [the Ayah] was revealed. This narration is supported by what is in al-Iḥtijāj, narrated from al-Hasan al-Mujtabā (peace be upon him) in a ḥadīth where he said: "As for you, O Walid ibn 'Uqbah, by Allah, I do not blame you for hating 'Ali, for he lashed you eighty stripes for drinking wine, and he killed your father by execution (صبراً) with his own hand on the Day of Badr. How can you revile him, when Allah named him a believer (مؤمناً) in ten Ayahs of the Quran, and named you a vicious person (فاسقاً), which is His saying: إِنْ جاءَكُمْ فاسِقٌ بِنَبَإٍ فَتَبَيَّنُوا (If a vicious person brings you a report, verify it) and the rest of the Ayah."

And al-Qummi [said]: It was revealed concerning 'A'ishah when she accused Mariyah al-Qibtiyyah and charged her with [adultery with] Jurayḥ al-Qibṭī. So the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم commanded the killing of Jurayḥ so that her lie would be exposed and she would repent from her sin. Her story has already passed in Sūrat al-Nūr.

(٧) وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّ فِيكُمْ رَسُولَ اللهِ لَوْ يُطِيعُكُمْ فِي كَثِيرٍ مِنَ الْأَمْرِ لَعَنِتُّمْ ** (7) And know that the Apostle of Allah is among you. Should He obey you in many matters, you would surely suffer hardship. (You would surely suffer hardship) means you would fall into hardship (العنت), which is difficulty (الجهد) and ruin (الهلاك). In this, there is an indication that some of them suggested to Him to cause trouble (الإيقاع) for Banu al-Mustaliq. ** وَلكِنَّ اللهَ حَبَّبَ إِلَيْكُمُ الْإِيمانَ وَزَيَّنَهُ فِي قُلُوبِكُمْ وَكَرَّهَ إِلَيْكُمُ الْكُفْرَ وَالْفُسُوقَ وَالْعِصْيانَ ** (7) But Allah has endeared faith to you and adorned it in your hearts, and He has made disbelief, transgression, and disobedience hateful to you. It is said that this is an address to the believers who did not commit that act and did not lie for their corrupt purpose (لغرضهم الفاسد), in order to praise them and implicitly censure those who did.

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): ** الْفُسُوقَ ** (transgression) means lying (الكذب).

In al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): ** حَبَّبَ إِلَيْكُمُ الْإِيمانَ وَزَيَّنَهُ فِي قُلُوبِكُمْ ** (He has endeared faith to you and adorned it in your hearts) means the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). ** وَكَرَّهَ إِلَيْكُمُ الْكُفْرَ وَالْفُسُوقَ وَالْعِصْيانَ ** (and He has made disbelief, transgression, and disobedience hateful to you) means the First, the Second, and the Third (الأوّل والثاني والثالث).

In al-Mahasin, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse, and it was said to him: "Do the servants have any role (صنع) in what Allah has endeared?" He replied: "No, and no dignity (ولا كرامة)."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): Religion (الدّين) is love (الحبّ), and love is religion.

In al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about love (الحبّ) and hatred (البغض), whether it is part of faith (الايمان). He replied: "Is faith anything but love and hatred?" Then He recited this verse:

** أُولئِكَ هُمُ الرَّاشِدُونَ ** (7) It is they who are the rightly guided. Meaning: Those with whom Allah has done that are the ones who have found the straight path (الطريق السويّ).

(٨) فَضْلاً مِنَ اللهِ وَنِعْمَةً وَاللهُ عَلِيمٌ ** (8) (as) a grace from Allah and a favour, and Allah is all-knowing, concerning the states of the believers and the degrees of superiority (التفاضل) among them. حَكِيمٌ ** when He grants superiority (يُفَضِّل) and bestows favor (يُنْعِم) upon them through divine success (التوفيق). (٩) وَإِنْ طائِفَتانِ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ اقْتَتَلُوا ** (9) If two groups of the faithful fight one another, they fought one another (تَقَاتَلُوا). The plural form is used considering the meaning, for every group (طَائِفَة) is a collective noun (جَمْع). فَأَصْلِحُوا بَيْنَهُما ** then make peace between them. by offering sincere advice (النصح) and calling them to the judgment (حُكْم) of Allah. فَإِنْ بَغَتْ إِحْداهُما عَلَى الْأُخْرى ** But if one of them aggresses against the other, it transgressed against it (تَعَدَّتْ عَلَيْهَا). فَقاتِلُوا الَّتِي تَبْغِي حَتَّى تَفِيءَ إِلى أَمْرِ اللهِ fight the one which aggresses until it returns to Allah’s ordinance. it returns to His judgment (حُكْم) and what He has commanded. فَإِنْ فاءَتْ فَأَصْلِحُوا بَيْنَهُما بِالْعَدْلِ Then, if it returns, make peace between them fairly, by settling the dispute (فَصْل) between them according to what Allah has judged. It was said that the restriction of reconciliation (الإصلاح) to justice (العدل) here is because it is a place where injustice (الحَيْف) is suspected, given that it occurs after fighting (المُقَاتَلَة). وَأَقْسِطُوا ** and act justly. and be just (وَاعْدِلُوا) in all matters. إِنَّ اللهَ يُحِبُّ الْمُقْسِطِينَ Indeed Allah loves the just ones.

It was said that [this Ayah] was revealed concerning a fight that occurred between the Aws and the Khazraj during his time, using palm branches (السَّعَف) and sandals (النِّعَال).

In al-Kafi, al-Tahdhib, and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq, from his father (peace be upon them both), in a tradition, he said: When this Ayah was revealed, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Indeed, among you is one who will fight after me over the interpretation (التأويل), just as I fought over the revelation (التنزيل)." He was asked who that was. He said: "The mender of the sandal (خَاصِف النَّعْل)," meaning the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). Then Ammar ibn Yasir said: "I fought with this banner alongside the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم three times, and this is the fourth. By Allah, even if they strike us until they drive us to the palm branches (السَّعَفَات) of Hajar, we know that we are upon the truth (الحَقّ) and they are upon falsehood (الْبَاطِل)." The conduct (السيرة) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) towards them was the same as the conduct of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم towards the people of Mecca on the Day of the Conquest of Mecca (فَتْح مَكَّة). For indeed, He صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم did not take their offspring (ذُرِّيَّة) captive, and He said: "Whoever locks his door is safe, and whoever lays down his weapon is safe, and whoever enters the house of Abu Sufyan is safe." Likewise, the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said on the Day of Basra (يَوْم الْبَصْرَة), proclaiming among them: "Do not take their offspring (ذُرِّيَّة) captive, do not finish off the wounded (جَرِيح), and do not pursue one who flees (مُدْبِر). Whoever locks his door and lays down his weapon is safe."

In al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): The interpretation (تأويل) of this Ayah only came on the Day of Basra (يَوْم الْبَصْرَة). They are the people of this Ayah, and they are those who aggressed (بَغَوْا) against the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). Thus, it was obligatory (الْوَاجِب) upon him to fight them and kill them until they returned to the ordinance (أَمْر) of Allah. And had they not returned, it would have been obligatory upon him, according to what Allah revealed, not to lift the sword from them until they returned and retracted their opinion, because they had pledged allegiance (بَايَعُوا) willingly, not reluctantly. And they are the aggressing group (الْفِئَة الْبَاغِيَة), as Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said. Therefore, it was obligatory upon the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) to act justly (يَعْدِل) towards them when He gained victory over them, just as the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم acted justly towards the people of Mecca, only granting them favor (مَنَّ) and pardoning them (عَفَا). And likewise, Ali, the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), did the same with the people of Basra when He gained victory over them, exactly as the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم did with the people of Mecca, step for step (حَذْو النَّعْل بِالنَّعْل).

(١٠) إِنَّمَا الْمُؤْمِنُونَ إِخْوَةٌ. (10) The believers are indeed brothers. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): They are the children of one father and one mother, and if a vein throbs for one of them, the others remain awake for him. And narrated from him (peace be upon him): The believer is the brother of the believer, his eye and his guide. He does not betray him, nor oppress him, nor find fault with him, nor make him a promise and then break it. And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The believer is the brother of the believer by his father and mother, because Allah created the believers from the clay (طينة) of Paradise and caused the fragrance (ريح) of Paradise to flow in their forms (صورهم). Therefore, they are brothers by father and mother. And in al-Basa'ir, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about the interpretation of this tradition: "The believer looks with the Light (نور) of Allah." He (peace be upon him) said: "Indeed, Allah created the believer from His Light (نوره) and dyed them with His Mercy (رحمته), and took their covenant (ميثاقهم) for us regarding the guardianship (الولاية) based on His knowledge (معرفته) on the Day He introduced Himself to them. Thus, the believer is the brother of the believer by his father and mother; his father is the Light (النّور) and his mother is the Mercy (الرحمة). And he only looks with that Light (النور) from which he was created." I say: And another aspect (وجه آخر) of the brotherhood of the believers is their attribution (انتسابهم) to the Prophet and the Successor (الوصيّ). For it has been narrated that he صلى الله عليه وآله said: "I and you, O Ali, are the two fathers (أبوا) of this nation (الأمة)." And another aspect is their attribution to faith (الايمان) which necessitates eternal life (الحياة الابديّة). فَأَصْلِحُوا بَيْنَ أَخَوَيْكُمْ. (So make peace between your two brothers.) In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The charity (صدقة) that Allah loves is reconciliation (إصلاح) between people when they become corrupt (تفاسدوا) and bringing them closer together when they become distant (تباعدوا). And narrated from him (peace be upon him): That I reconcile between two people is more beloved to me than that I give two دينارين in charity. And narrated from him (peace be upon him): He said to Mufaddal: "If you see a dispute (منازعة) between two of our followers (شيعتنا), then ransom it (فافتدها) from my wealth (مالي)." And in a narration, he said: "The reconciler (المصلح) is not a liar (كذّاب)." ** وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ ** (and be wary of Allah) regarding the contravention of His ruling (حكمه) and negligence therein, لَعَلَّكُمْ تُرْحَمُونَ (that you may be granted mercy) based on your wariness (تقواكم).

(١١)يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لا يَسْخَرْ قَوْمٌ مِنْ قَوْمٍ عَسى أَنْ يَكُونُوا خَيْراً مِنْهُمْ وَلا نِساءٌ مِنْ نِساءٍ عَسى أَنْ يَكُنَّ خَيْراً مِنْهُنَ (O you who have faith! Let not any people ridicule another people, for they may be better than them, nor let women ridicule other women, for they may be better than them.) That is, let not some of the believing men and believing women ridicule others, since the one ridiculed may be better in the sight of Allah than the ridiculer.

Al-Qummi (القمّيّ): It was revealed concerning Safiyyah bint Huyayy ibn Akhtab, who was the wife of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. This was because 'A'ishah and Hafsah used to harm her and insult her, saying to her: "O daughter of the Jewess!" So she complained about that to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. He said to her: "Why do you not answer them?" She said: "With what, O Messenger of Allah?" He said: "Say: 'Indeed, my father is Harun, the Prophet of Allah (نبيّ الله), and my uncle is Musa, the Speaker of Allah (كليم الله), and my husband is Muhammad, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. So what do you deny concerning me?'" So she said this to them. They then said: "This is your uncle, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله." So Allah revealed concerning that: يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لا يَسْخَرْ (O you who have faith! Let not any people ridicule) the Ayah.

وَلا تَلْمِزُوا أَنْفُسَكُمْ (nor defame one another) And let not some of you find fault with others.

وَلا تَنابَزُوا بِالْأَلْقابِ (nor revile one another by [calling foul] names.) And let not some of you call others by an evil nickname (لقب السوء).

بِئْسَ الِاسْمُ الْفُسُوقُ بَعْدَ الْإِيمانِ (Evil is the name of ungodliness after faith.) That is, wretched is the elevated mention (الذكر المرتفع) for the believers—namely, that they should be mentioned by ungodliness (الفسق) after their entry into faith (الايمان) and their becoming known by it.

وَمَنْ لَمْ يَتُبْ (And whoever does not repent) from what He forbade,

فَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الظَّالِمُونَ (it is they who are the wrongdoers.) by placing disobedience (العصيان) in the place of obedience (الطاعة) and exposing the soul to punishment (العذاب).

(١٢) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اجْتَنِبُوا كَثِيراً مِنَ الظَّنِ ** (O you who have faith! Avoid much suspicion) Be on one side of it, and the ambiguity (إبهام) of "much" (الكثير) is so that one takes precaution regarding every suspicion (ظنّ) and reflects until he knows which category it belongs to. إِنَّ بَعْضَ الظَّنِّ إِثْمٌ ** (Indeed, some suspicion is a sin) The sin (الإثم) is the transgression (الذنب) by which one deserves punishment (العقوبة). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq, from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon them both), he said: "Place the affair of your brother upon its best interpretation until something comes to you that overturns it. And do not suspect ill of a word that came from your brother when you can find a good interpretation (محملاً) for it." In Nahj al-Balagha: "If righteousness prevails over the time and its people, and then a man harbors ill suspicion (أساء الظنّ) toward a man from whom no disgrace (خزية) has appeared, he has certainly committed injustice (ظلم). And if corruption prevails over the time and its people, and then a man harbors good suspicion (أحسن الظنّ) toward a man, he has certainly been deceived (غرّر)." ** وَلا تَجَسَّسُوا ** (and do not spy) And do not search for the faults (عورات) of the believers. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "Do not seek out the slips (عثرات) of the believers, for whoever follows the slips of his brother, Allah will follow his slip, and whomsoever Allah follows his slip, He will disgrace him, even if it is inside the depths of his house." ** وَلا يَغْتَبْ بَعْضُكُمْ بَعْضاً ** (nor backbite one another.) And let not some of you mention others with evil in their absence (غيبته). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about backbiting (الغيبة), and He said: "It is that you say about your brother concerning his religion what he has not done, and you spread about him an affair that Allah has concealed for him, unless a hadd (حدّ) punishment has been established against him concerning it." And in a narration: "As for the apparent matter in him, such as sharpness (الحدّة) or haste (العجلة), then no (that is not backbiting)." And narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): "Whoever mentions a man behind his back with what is true about him, which people know, has not backbitten him. And whoever mentions him behind his back with what is true about him, which people do not know, has backbitten him. And whoever mentions him with what is not true about him has certainly slandered him (بهته)." In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "Whoever deals with people and does not wrong them, speaks to them and does not lie to them, and promises them and does not break his promise, he is among those whose chivalry (مروّته) is complete, whose justice (عدالته) is manifest, whose brotherhood (أخوّته) is obligatory, and whose backbiting (غيبته) is forbidden (حرمت)." A similar narration is found in al-Kafi and al-Khisal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him). And in al-Majma', in the hadith: "Say about the transgressor (الفاسق) what is true about him so that people may beware of him." And narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: "Beware of backbiting (الغيبة), for backbiting is more severe than fornication (الزنا)." Then He said: "Indeed, a man commits fornication and repents, and Allah accepts his repentance. But the perpetrator of backbiting is not forgiven unless his victim forgives him." A similar narration is found in al-Khisal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him). ** ** أَيُحِبُّ أَحَدُكُمْ أَنْ يَأْكُلَ لَحْمَ أَخِيهِ مَيْتاً فَكَرِهْتُمُوهُ ** (Would any of you like to eat his dead brother’s flesh? You would hate it.) This is a metaphor (تمثيل) for what the backbiter gains from the honor (عرض) of the backbitten, presented in the most repulsive manner (على افحش وجه), coupled with the exaggerations of the affirmative interrogation (الاستفهام المقرّر), the attribution of the verb to "anyone" (أحد) for generalization (للتعميم), the linking of love (المحبّة) to something that is the utmost disgrace (غاية الكرامة), the representation of backbiting (الاغتياب) as eating human flesh, making the eaten person a dead brother, and following that up with the saying فَكَرِهْتُمُوهُ (You would hate it) as an affirmation (تقريراً) and verification (تحقيقاً) of that. And it was read with tashdid (مشدّداً). وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ إِنَّ اللهَ تَوَّابٌ رَحِيمٌ ** (Be wary of Allah. Indeed Allah is all-clement, all-merciful.) For whoever is wary of what He has forbidden and repents from what he has neglected (فرط منه). In al-Jawami', it is narrated: That Abu Bakr and Umar sent Salman to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله to bring them food. So he [Salman] sent him [the messenger] to Usama ibn Zayd, who was the treasurer (خازن) of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله for his baggage (رحله). Usama said: "I do not have anything." So he [Salman] returned to them. They said: "Usama was stingy (بخل). If we had sent Salman to the well of Sumayha (بئر سميحة), its water would have sunk down (لغار ماؤها)." Then they went to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. He said to them: "Why do I see the greenness of meat in your mouths?" They said: "O Messenger of Allah, we have not eaten meat today." He said: "You have been enjoying (تفكّهون) the flesh of Salman and Usama." So, [the verse] was revealed.

(١٣) يا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ إِنَّا خَلَقْناكُمْ مِنْ ذَكَرٍ وَأُنْثى ** (13) O mankind! Indeed, We created you from a male and a female Adam and Eve, وَجَعَلْناكُمْ شُعُوباً وَقَبائِلَ and made you nations and tribes Al-Qummi said: The nations (الشعوب) are the non-Arabs (العجم) and the tribes (القبائل) are the Arabs. And he narrated it in al-Majma' from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him). لِتَعارَفُوا that you may recognize one another. So that some of you may know others, not for boasting about fathers and tribes. إِنَّ أَكْرَمَكُمْ عِنْدَ اللهِ أَتْقاكُمْ ** Indeed the noblest of you in the sight of Allah is the most Godwary among you. For indeed, through piety (التقوى), souls are perfected and individuals are distinguished; so whoever desires honor, let him seek it through it (piety).

Al-Qummi: This is a refutation against those who boast about lineage (الأحساب) and ancestry (الأنساب). And the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said on the Day of the Conquest of Mecca: "O mankind! Indeed, Allah has removed from you, through Islam, the arrogance (نخوة) of the Age of Ignorance (الجاهلية) and its boasting about its fathers. Indeed, Arab-ness (العربيّة) is not a father's door (باب والد); rather, it is a speaking tongue (لسان ناطق). Whoever speaks it is an Arab. Know that you are all from Adam, and Adam is from dust, and Indeed the noblest of you in the sight of Allah is the most Godwary among you."

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم): Allah the Almighty will say on the Day of Resurrection: "I commanded you, but you neglected what I entrusted to you concerning it, and you elevated your ancestries (أنسابكم). Today, I elevate My lineage (نسبي) and abase your ancestries. Where are the Godwary (المتّقون)? Indeed the noblest of you in the sight of Allah is the most Godwary among you."

In al-Faqih, narrated from al-Sadiq, from his father, from his grandfather (peace be upon them): The Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: "The most Godwary (أتقى) of people is he who speaks the truth concerning what is for him and what is against him."

In al-I'tiqadat, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about the Almighty's saying: إِنَّ أَكْرَمَكُمْ عِنْدَ اللهِ أَتْقاكُمْ ** Indeed the noblest of you in the sight of Allah is the most Godwary among you. He said: "The one among you who acts most according to al-Taqiyyah (التقيّة)."

And in al-Ikmal, a similar narration is reported from al-Rida (peace be upon him). ** إِنَّ اللهَ عَلِيمٌ ** Indeed Allah is All-knowing, concerning you, خَبِيرٌ ** All-aware, of your inner states (بواطنكم).

(١٤) قالَتِ الْأَعْرابُ آمَنَّا ** (14) The Bedouins say, 'We have faith.' It is said that this was revealed concerning a group of people from Banu Asad who came to Medina during a year of drought and displayed the two testimonies (الشهادتين). They used to say to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله: "We came to you with heavy burdens and families, and we did not fight you as Banu Fulan fought you," intending thereby to receive charity (الصدقة) and showing favor (يمنّون). قُلْ لَمْ تُؤْمِنُوا ** [Say, 'You have not had faith'] in Him, since faith (الإيمان) is affirmation (تصديق) accompanied by trust (ثقة) and tranquility of the heart (طمأنينة قلب), and this has not been attained by you. وَلكِنْ قُولُوا أَسْلَمْنا ** [but say, 'We have submitted.'] For Islam (الإسلام) is submission (انقياد), entering into peace (السلم), displaying the testimony (إظهار الشهادة), and abandoning warfare, which is what this implies. The structure of the speech should have been: "Do not say, 'We have faith,' وَلكِنْ قُولُوا أَسْلَمْنا " or "You have not had faith, but you have submitted." However, He deviated from that structure to this one, avoiding the prohibition of claiming faith (الإيمان) while affirming their Islam (إسلام), even though the condition for its legal validity (شرعاً) was lacking.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, Islam (الإسلام) precedes faith (الإيمان). Based on Islam, they inherit from one another and intermarry, while based on faith, they are rewarded. And narrated from Him (peace be upon him): Faith (الإيمان) is affirmation by the tongue (الإقرار باللّسان), conviction in the heart (عقد في القلب), and action by the limbs (عمل بالأركان). Faith is part of faith, and it is a domain (دار). Likewise, Islam (الإسلام) is a domain, and disbelief (الكفر) is a domain. A servant may be a Muslim (مسلماً) before he is a believer (مؤمناً), but he cannot be a believer until he is a Muslim. Thus, Islam precedes faith, and it shares in faith. If the servant commits a major sin (كبيرة من كبائر المعاصي) or a minor sin (صغيرة من صغاير المعاصي) which Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has forbidden, he is outside of faith, the name of faith (اسم الايمان) drops from him, but the name of Islam (اسم الإسلام) remains fixed upon him. If he repents and seeks forgiveness, he returns to the domain of faith (دار الايمان). Nothing removes him to disbelief (الكفر) except denial (الجحود) and deeming the forbidden lawful (الاستحلال). (The rest of the narration).

And in a narration: Islam is the outward aspect upon which people rely: the testimony that there is no god except Allah and that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, establishing the prayer (اقام الصلاة), giving the Zakat (إيتاء الزكاة), performing the pilgrimage to the House (حجّ البيت), and fasting the month of Ramadan (صيام شهر رمضان). This is Islam. And faith (الإيمان) is the knowledge (معرفة) of this matter (هذا الامر) along with this [Islam]. If he affirms these [pillars] but does not know this matter, he is a Muslim (مسلماً) but misguided (ضالاً).

And narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The Muslim (المسلم) is he from whose tongue and hand the Muslims are safe, and the believer (المؤمن) is he whom the Muslims trust with their wealth and their lives. (The rest of the narration).

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, He said: "Islam is public (علانية), and faith (الإيمان) is in the heart," and He pointed to His chest. ** وَلَمَّا يَدْخُلِ الْإِيمانُ فِي قُلُوبِكُمْ ** [and faith has not yet entered into your hearts] is a temporal limit for their saying [i.e., their claim of faith]. ** وَإِنْ تُطِيعُوا اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ ** [But if you obey Allah and His Apostle] with sincerity (الإخلاص) and abandoning hypocrisy (النّفاق), لا يَلِتْكُمْ مِنْ أَعْمالِكُمْ ** [He will not stint you [of the reward] of your deeds]—He will not diminish anything from their rewards—شَيْئاً ** [of anything], derived from al-layt (اللّيت). It is also recited lā ya’litukum (لا يالتكم), derived from al-alt (الالت), which is a dialectical variant thereof. ** إِنَّ اللهَ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ ** [Indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful.] Indeed Allah is all-forgiving (غَفُورٌ) regarding what has been neglected by the obedient, all-merciful (رَحِيمٌ) by bestowing His favor upon them.

(١٥) إِنَّمَا الْمُؤْمِنُونَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا بِاللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ ثُمَّ لَمْ يَرْتابُوا ** (15) The faithful are only those who have believed in Allah and His Apostle and then have not doubted, They did not doubt, وَجاهَدُوا بِأَمْوالِهِمْ وَأَنْفُسِهِمْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ and have waged jihad with their possessions and their lives in the way of Allah. في طاعته in His obedience. أُولئِكَ هُمُ الصَّادِقُونَ It is they who are the truthful. الذين صدقوا في ادعاء الايمان those who were truthful in claiming faith. Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: It was revealed concerning the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him).

(١٦) قُلْ أَتُعَلِّمُونَ اللهَ بِدِينِكُمْ ** (16) Say, 'Are you informing Allah about your religion? Are you informing Him of it by your saying, "We have believed," ** وَاللهُ يَعْلَمُ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَما فِي الْأَرْضِ وَاللهُ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ ** and Allah knows whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth, and Allah has knowledge of all things.' Nothing hidden is concealed from Him. And this is declaring them ignorant and a rebuke. It is narrated that when the preceding Ayah was revealed, they came and swore that they were believers (مؤمنون), convinced (معتقدون), so this [Ayah] was revealed.

(١٧) ** يَمُنُّونَ عَلَيْكَ أَنْ أَسْلَمُوا ** (17) They regard it as a favour to you that they have embraced Islam. They consider their embracing of Islam a favor (منّة) upon you ** قُلْ لا تَمُنُّوا عَلَيَّ إِسْلامَكُمْ ** Say, 'Do not regard your Islam as a favour to me.' meaning: by your embracing of Islam ** بَلِ اللهُ يَمُنُّ عَلَيْكُمْ أَنْ هَداكُمْ لِلْإِيمانِ ** Rather, Allah favours you in that He has guided you to the faith, according to what you claim, even though guidance (الهداية) does not necessitate being guided (الاهتداء) ** إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ ** if you are truthful.' in claiming faith (الايمان).

Al-Qummi (reports): It was revealed concerning Uthman on the Day of the Trench (يوم الخندق). That was because he passed by Ammar ibn Yasir while he was digging the Trench, and dust had risen from the excavation. Uthman placed his sleeve over his nose and passed by. So Ammar said: "He who builds the mosques and prays in them, bowing (راكعاً) and prostrating (ساجداً), is not equal to him who passes by the dust, turning away (حائداً), disregarding it (يعرض عنه), denying (جاحداً), and being obstinate (معانداً)." Uthman turned to him and said: "O son of the black woman (يا ابن السوداء), do you mean me?" Then he came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله and said: "We did not enter with you so that our honor (أعراضنا) should be insulted." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said to him: "I have absolved you of your Islam, so go." Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), revealed: ** يَمُنُّونَ عَلَيْكَ أَنْ أَسْلَمُوا ** (They regard it as a favour to you that they have embraced Islam) up to His saying ** صادِقِينَ ** (if you are truthful), meaning: they are not truthful.

(١٨) إِنَّ اللهَ يَعْلَمُ غَيْبَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ (18) Indeed Allah knows the unseen of the heavens and the earth. that which is hidden in them وَاللهُ بَصِيرٌ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ and Allah is watchful of what you do. in your secrecy and your openness, so how can that which is in your consciences (ضمائركم) be hidden from Him? And it was read with the yā’ (الياء).

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever recites Surah al-Hujurat every night or every day will be among the visitors (زوّار) of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.
